TextENGNew: Shino raised the small dish to her lips and tilted it.
I watched that brief moment unfold, my body rigid with tension.
``How... is it?``
The atmosphere in the kitchen was taut as a bowstring.
A cold and quiet presence hung over the room, like the surface of a lake without any waves.
I was waiting for Shino to pass her judgment, like a prisoner on trial.
And as time continued passing after my question, my doubt turned to anxiety. And just as my anxiety was beginning to turn into fear...
``Hanyuu.``
Shino finally opened her mouth, and...
``...Yeah, I think it's good.``
My jaw dropped for a moment, as I struggled to process what I'd just heard, but my bewilderment quickly turned into heartfelt joy.
That was the first passing grade I'd ever received from Shino...!
``R-really?``
``Yes, this is perfect.@k@vS16/00/DS43030127. I can proudly say that it's delicious, not merely edible as it's been up until now.``
Shino gave me a thumbs-up as she said that.
That wasn't a certification just for matching her tastes.
It was a sign that I'd passed as a wife... a human wife of this world.
``Finally... I've finally made it this far!@k@vS16/45/DS43071192. This is the fruit of my refusal to be afraid of failure!@k@vS16/45/DS43071193. And above all else, the power of my love for Riku!!``
``Well, um... I mean, maybe you're right, but...``

``I'd also like you to remember from time to time how I taste-tested each one, encountered many failures,@k@vS16/00/DS43030130. and sometimes consumed poison so utterly vile I was convinced it had come from the cauldron of Hell itself! Teehee.``

``That's right... in the first place, I'm a woman!@k@vS16/45/DS43071195. In other words, it is a self-evident truth that my sensitivities are better adapted for housework than a man like Riku... my natural providence!``

``Aaah, in hindsight, I felt so ashamed at how my husband was better at cooking than me! Just how long did I vow to redouble my efforts...?@k@vS16/45/DS43071197. But now I say farewell to those humiliating days...!``
``And hello to my glorious futureeee!!``
``Um, Hanyuu...?@k@vS16/00/DS43030132. Hey, come back...``
``...Or not. Heehee.@k@vS16/45/DS43071200. I'm just joking.@k@vS16/45/DS43071201. I can never repay you enough for all that you've done for me.``
``...You're becoming more and more like Riku, huh?``
...Quite some time had passed since the day I discovered my pregnancy.
It would only be a few months until the anniversary of that summer day arrived.
My stomach had grown considerably bigger as a sign the baby was growing healthy. Thanks to that, I felt a lot heavier than I used to, and it was a challenge just to take a single step.
``Speaking of which, where is Riku?``
``Ah, he's out mowing the grass by the shrine.@k@vS16/45/DS43071203. ...He was being such a nag that I threw him out.``
After all, if I so much as took even a single step outside these days, he'd come running to me like it was the end of the world.
``Please be careful, Hanyuu!@k@vS16/46/DS43020648. If you need something, I'll handle it!``
``Stop, please stay still in the house!@k@vS16/46/DS43020650. What would happen if you tripped?!``
He was being so overprotective of me that it made me wonder if he'd forgotten just what I was.
But by no means did I hate his consideration. If anything, it warmed my heart greatly... and made me so happy.
I gently rubbed my stomach and felt it.
I was self-conscious of the fact... that our child was growing on the other side of this thin wall.
Lately, it had been kicking from time to time.
Every single time made me aware of how a new life was thriving within me, filling my heart with so much joy.
It wouldn't be too long until the due date. It seemed the child would arrive while the cherry blossoms were still in bloom.
``...Aah, what a lovely smell.``
``Oh, Riku.@k@vS16/45/DS43071206. Good work.``
Riku opened the kitchen's back door while wiping his sweat with a towel. I was just about to call for him anyway, so this was perfect timing.
``Isn't this great, Riku?@k@vS16/00/DS43030136. It looks like you're in for a feast today.``
``Wait, is that true?@k@vS16/46/DS43020653. Whew, that's good to know.@k@vS16/46/DS43020654. But I'm fine with anything Hanyuu cooks, you know?``
``...You don't believe it, do you?``
Just you watch. I swear I'll give you the shock of your life.
I told myself that, then I forced Riku out of the kitchen and went to work on the ingredients in front of me.
Lunch today was the happiest one of my life.
Riku, who I'd thought was a light eater up until now, consumed my meal with gusto.
``Th-this is delicious!@k@vS16/46/DS43020656. It's wonderful! Seriously!``
``D-do you really think so...?``

``Yes!@k@vS16/46/DS43020658. ...Aah, what a lucky man I am!@k@vS16/46/DS43020659. I'd thought anything was great as long as it was cooked by you, but to think you'd achieve such heights, such depth, such flavor!!``
``Oh Heaven, oh Earth, and all gods in creation!@k@vS16/46/DS43020661. In this moment, I am grateful to you from the bottom of my heart!@k@vS16/46/DS43020662. Thank youuuu!!``
``Agh, shut up!@k@vS16/00/DS43030138. Keep quiet during a meal!@k@vS16/00/DS43030139. What are you, a child?!``
...It was from that reaction I realized that Riku had been really forcing himself to eat my crappy cooking up until now, and honestly, my heart stung.
Even so, when I saw Riku looking so delighted to the extent it exasperated Shino... I was beyond happy.

``Haah... I really am a lucky man.@k@vS16/46/DS43020664. I wouldn't care if I died right now!@k@vS16/46/DS43020665. ...Wait, I take that back.@k@vS16/46/DS43020666. At least let me live until this village is destroyed!!``
``Don't jinx it!``
``Sh-sheesh... you're taking this too far... Riku.``
``...I knew I should have turned down lunch with you two.@k@vS16/00/DS43030142. The atmosphere alone is making me full.``
``Ahahaha...``
...That's right. I'll get better and better, and make more delicious meals for Riku.
And for our soon to be born child too, of course.
Then we'll be a loving family that'll make every meal a lively one.
Always...
As many days possible for Riku and my child...
The early blooming cherry blossoms, dyed by the setting sun, rustled as the wind toyed with them.
The blossoming flowers would likely be in full bloom soon as well. And soon, the chilly air will surely be replaced with warm, comfortable air.
Enjoying the changing of the seasons... What a natural thing to do.
Right now, I deeply treasured all the things I didn't know before meeting Riku.
``Riku, Shino, thank you.@k@vS16/45/DS43071212. I'm sure I would not be who I am right now without the both of you.@k@vS16/45/DS43071213. Please let me offer my heartfelt gratitude.``
``Ahahaha, what's this all about?@k@vS16/00/DS43030144. Where did that come from?``
``I truly am a lucky person.@k@vS16/45/DS43071215. Up until now, I never thought I'd be with another.``
``Hanyuu...``
Riku gripped my left hand, while Shino followed suit and gently wrapped her hands around my right.
``I'm the one who should be grateful.@k@vS16/46/DS43020669. I have you as my life partner, and you make each day unbelievably happy.@k@vS16/46/DS43020670. Thank you, Hanyuu.``

``Heehee... come on, don't be a stranger.@k@vS16/00/DS43030146. Some things just go without saying!@k@vS16/00/DS43030147. Because the fact that I can do something for my beloved friends... makes me truly happy, as well.``
``Riku... Shino...``
Riku was smiling, while Shino was embarrassed.
Their reactions were different, but the feelings they conveyed were the same, and gentle.
I was also secretly grateful to the fate that allowed me to encounter these wonderful people.
``Besides, soon enough it'll be the four of us, not three, right?@k@vS16/00/DS43030149. Ah, but I guess that'd be seven if you included my husband and sons.``

``Sounds like things are about to get lively.@k@vS16/46/DS43020672. ...I know, why don't you bring your whole family along next time?@k@vS16/46/DS43020673. Us two families can watch the cherry blossoms together.``

``Ah, that sounds wonderful!@k@vS16/00/DS43030151. Ryou... Oh, that's my husband.@k@vS16/00/DS43030152. Anyway, he doesn't talk much, so he may seem difficult to approach, but he truly loves booze.@k@vS16/00/DS43030153. And Kazuya and Shouji are such a rambunctious pair.``
``Heehee...``
...They were right.
In just a few days, me and Riku's child would be here with us too.
We'd have another person, when I was already so happy surrounded by such kind people.
I was sure we'd be even happier.
I gently placed my hands on my stomach, as if to tell my beloved child...
That there were so many people who wanted to meet and spend time with it...
Just then...
My stomach throbbed with a stabbing pain.
``...Hmm? Is something the matter, Hanyuu?``
``I-it hurts...``
``Huh? No way! Are you having contractions?!``
``I-I don't know, ugh...!``
The pain throbbed through me again.
I bent over, clutching at my stomach.
It felt as though my organs were being stabbed from the inside out...
``Ghh... aah, aghhhh!``
``Hanyuu! Are you all right?!``
I could hear Riku calling out to me in concern, so I tried to respond... but all that escaped my throat was a scream.
(I can't speak...!)
My body was wracked with pain so intense that it wouldn't do what I told it to.
It hurt to even adjust my pose slightly, so I stayed in the same position, simply enduring the pain with all my being.
``...Agh, aghhhhhhhhhhhhhhh!!``
``This is bad, it looks like she really is having the baby.@k@vS16/00/DS43030156. Riku! Get a futon in the living room and boil some water in the kettle!``
``Aah, wait, but I can't leave Hanyuu like thi...``
``Don't panic!@k@vS16/00/DS43030158. You're a father, aren't you? So calm yourself a little, and get to it!``
``...Y-yes, understood.@k@vS16/46/DS43020678. A futon and hot water, yes? I'll get them ready right away!``
``Hanyuu?@k@vS16/00/DS43030160. Hanyuu, can you hear me?``
I nodded in response... That was the best I could manage right now.
``Riku's getting a futon right now, so once he's done, you'll have to move there.``
``I-I can't... I've never felt pain like this before... Ugh!``
``It's all right, the pain will fade relatively soon.@k@vS16/00/DS43030163. So don't give in!``
``Ugh... Shino, Shino...!``
``Hang in there!@k@vS16/00/DS43030165. You're about to become a mother, aren't you?!``
My hazy mind reacted to Shino's encouraging words.
...A mother.
That's right, I had to hang in there.
I looked down at my stomach, shaking with intense pain.
The child was fighting a desperate battle of their own in there. To reach the outside world. To reach us.
In that case, this sort of pain was nothing.
...I'll do it.
I'll give birth to our healthy child...!
``How is the child's growth?``
``It's going very well.@k@vS16/00/DS43050277. Your child is growing healthily.``
``I see, that's great.@k@vS16/45/DS43071229. ...But you know, Ryuun-Ohc...``
``What is it?``
``...You're not saying anything, huh?``
``I fail to understand the intent of your question.@k@vS16/00/DS43050280. What exactly do you mean, Hanyuu?``
I swallowed the words I was about to say, and pondered the meaning of her question a little.
...Was she simply seeking an explanation like always? Or...
``In all honesty, I never thought you'd be so supportive of me having a baby.``

``Since I can't move properly now that I've gotten so big, it'll be difficult to fight any Grifys should the need arise.@k@vS16/45/DS43071234. You're in charge of Grifys surveillance and countermeasures, so I figured this wouldn't be ideal for you.``
``Hanyuu, you are mistaken about one thing.``
``About what?``
``I am Ryuun-Ohc.@k@vS16/00/DS43050283. My role to observe Grifys is superseded by my role to control the gate that links to the Ryuun's former homeworld, and to link the scattered Ryuun.``
``Hence why I was given the name of Ryuun-Ohc: Brethren linker.``
``Therefore, I celebrate not just the Ryuun scattered across the land, but the birth of new Ryuun from the bottom of my heart.``
``...I see. Thanks.``
``And there is one more reason why I am not opposed to your pregnancy.``
``Another reason?@k@vS16/45/DS43071238. What's that?``
``I am happy, Hanyuu.@k@vS16/00/DS43050288. Happy that I can witness you living such a blissful life full of such emotions...``
``Ugh, agh, kwaaaaghhhh!``
I screamed, unable to bear the pain that was splitting my body in two.
The intervals between contractions had gradually grown shorter. Half a day had already passed.
The sun had long set, and dawn would be making its appearance soon enough.
``Hanyuu...@k@vS16/46/DS43020680. Please hang in there, Hanyuu...``
Riku called out to me while gripping my right hand, and I responded by gripping his hand back.
...I never thought giving birth would be this painful.
I figured that while there would be some pain, I could take it.
I'd fought countless battles as a Jedha up until now, and suffered many injuries.
Pain got in the way of battle. So in my many years of fighting I'd tried learning all sorts of ways to dull the pain.
However...!
``Uwaah, gwaaaaghhhhhhhhhh!``
My whole body felt like it was getting torn apart.
The intense pain brought forth by that sensation couldn't be compared to wounds suffered on the battlefield.

Internal and external pain were different to begin with, so they probably shouldn't be compared. But unlike the external pain I'd gotten used to, this internal pain I was experiencing for the first time was far too brutal.
My vision flickered.
I felt like I was going to pass out from the excruciating agony, but that pain snapped me back awake.
After that repeated over and over again, the pain grew even worse.
``Hang in there, Hanyuu! Just a little more!``
Shino, who was serving as my midwife, shouted to encourage me.
Riku, who'd been holding my hand this whole time, cheered me on as well.
They'd been with me ever since I went into labor, meaning they'd been doing this for about 12 hours.
So, for their sake too, I couldn't give in.
Almost there. The baby's almost here...!
``Yes, I'm right here, Hanyuu.``
``Please... please hold my hand...!``
``Yes, I'm already holding it.``
``No, harder, hold it tighter!``
``Right!``
Riku gripped both of my hands tight.
When I opened my nearly shut eyelids, I found Riku looking straight at me.
His brow was furrowed with concern. It seemed his soft nature was on full display.
When I saw his face, a mysterious sense of ease came over me.
...It's all right.
Since I can see him so close, I'm sure there's nothing to worry about...
``Ghh, uwaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaghhhhhhhhhhhhhh!!``
I screamed my lungs out.
I pushed with all my might. My nails dug into my palms and drew blood, but that pain was drowned out by even greater pain.
My world went white. All sensations apart from pain disappeared, and then...
...The long-awaited moment finally arrived.
I faintly heard Shino's voice.
There was a sense of emptiness in my abdomen. The feeling that something that had been there up until now was gone.
The immense pain that'd wracked my body until a moment ago was now just a faint throb.
The baby's cries echoed loudly through the house.
Thank goodness.
I managed to become a mother...
``You did amazing, Hanyuu!``
I answered Riku while catching my breath.
Our hands were still firmly clasped together.
``Hanyuu! Riku!@k@vS16/00/DS43030170. It's a healthy girl!``
``That's right!@k@vS16/00/DS43030172. Go on, hold her!``
Shino was all smiles as she held a promptly washed baby, her body wrapped in a cloth so she wouldn't get cold.
(This baby is me and Riku's child...)
My heart nearly burst with joy. Driven by the urge to hold my baby right away, I forced my tired body to move and reached out towards Shino.
However...
My arms froze halfway there.
Shino cocked her head in confusion. Riku was also watching on with confusion.
My daughter was wailing in Shino's arms.
I wanted to see her now. To hold her in my arms.
To celebrate her birth with Riku.
...But what if...
A grim possibility flashed through my head, and I violently shook my head in denial.
``What's the matter?@k@vS16/46/DS43020686. Can you not move yet?``
``...I'm scared.``
``Hanyuu...?``
``I'm... scared.@k@vS16/45/DS43071254. ...Please, tell me.@k@vS16/45/DS43071255. Does the baby... have horns?``
I couldn't help but ask.
I was scared. So, so scared.
Riku said it would be all right. That he'd protect me and our child.
Knowing him, he was prepared to make good on his word and do anything for our daughter. Even if it meant literally abandoning something...
But in the end, that hadn't been enough to resolve any of my hesitations about the birth.
The reason I'd once considered aborting the child was so that she and Riku wouldn't be ostracized by the village. So that no one would get hurt. That was all.
And that was why I was scared.
If this child had horns, then she and I couldn't stay in the village...
``I told you, didn't I?@k@vS16/46/DS43020690. I will protect the both of you whether she has horns or not.``
``But that would mean you getting hurt, wouldn't it?!@k@vS16/45/DS43071258. I don't want that!``
``...Thank you.@k@vS16/46/DS43020692. I really am a lucky man to have such a caring wife.``
``Don't tease me, please!@k@vS16/45/DS43071260. Enough of that, what about the baby?!@k@vS16/45/DS43071261. Please, hurry up and tell me!``
Once I voiced my anxiety, Riku slowly opened his mouth.
``She has... no horns.``
``The baby doesn't have horns, Hanyuu.``
Riku's words slowly worked their way into my exhausted mind.
No horns.
This girl could live as a human, without ever suffering as I did.
And that meant...
Neither Riku, nor the baby, nor anyone would have to get hurt...
``Here, Hanyuu.``
Shino's words brought me back to reality.
She was stretching her hands out to me... holding a swaddled baby.
I timidly, awkwardly, and anxiously took her, then carefully held her in my arms.
She was crying, making her existence known to the world.
She wailed so loudly it made me wonder where in that tiny body of hers she was producing such strength.
When I shifted my gaze up from the baby's tearstained face, I saw that there was nothing on her head.
There was no need to worry anymore.
The worst possible future, where I hurt Riku and the baby, had vanished...
``...Uwaah... uwaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!!``
I cried loud enough to match the baby.
This child wouldn't have to suffer as I had.
Riku wouldn't have to make an enemy of the village.
No one would have to get hurt...!
``...Good job, Hanyuu.@k@vS16/46/DS43020696. And... thank you.``
``Uwaah, aaah, hic, aaaaaaaaah!``
Riku gently embraced me as I continued crying endlessly.
His arms were heated with that human warmth I'd never felt until I met him.
He gave me a place to belong when even I, being neither human nor Ryuun, didn't know what I was.
And now there was someone else who'd given me a place to belong.
This child crying in my arms just like me...
And so, I cried into Riku's chest for a while, then looked up.
``Are you sure you're all right?``
``...Yes, I'm fine.``
I slowly moved out of his grasp.
My face must have looked horrible from the exhausting labor and the crying. But since that was the result of me doing my best for my daughter, I was perhaps even proud of that now.
``Come to think of it, what should we do about her name?``
``Her... name?@k@vS16/45/DS43071268. ...Ah, right.@k@vS16/45/DS43071269. We never did get around to considering that.@k@vS16/45/DS43071270. What kind of name shall we go with...?``
Just as I was considering leaving the thinking up to Riku like always...
I looked up at the sudden glitter of light and turned toward it. Riku and Shino quickly followed suit, and saw it.
...It was the sunrise.
The sun was poking in through a gap in the ridges. It banished the darkness and restored brilliance to the formerly colorless world.
``...Hanyuu, Riku.@k@vS16/00/DS43030177. Look at that.``
I looked in the direction Shino was indicating, and saw...
It was a blizzard.
A dancing pink blizzard.
The sun was shining on a cherry tree in full bloom, blossoming in full glory as if to celebrate the new life.
Some of them had half-bloomed by yesterday, but now they were all in full bloom as if it were the height of spring.
Just then, a single cherry blossom petal crossed my line of sight.
The lightly dancing petal fluttered around us all... and landed on the baby's forehead.
Just then, I recalled my conversation with her.
In addition to Riku and Shino, there was one more ``person`` who thought about me and made me happy.
...I'd made up my mind.
That's right, this child's name would be...
``If you take the characters for ``cherry blossom`` and ``petal`` and put them together, you get the name ``Ouka``. What do you think about that?``
Riku looked toward the garden when he heard what I had to say.
He gazed at the blooming cherry tree, then answered with a smile.
``It's a fine name.``
Riku nodded with satisfaction on his face.
This scenery blossomed in full glory the moment this child... Ouka was born. I could think of no other name more fitting for her.
And what's more...
``Actually, the name Ouka has one additional meaning.``
``Oh? What kind of meaning?@k@vS16/00/DS43030179. Tell me, tell me!``
Shino leaned forward and asked.
I chuckled when I saw her rejoicing as if this were all happening to her.
``My original language has a word called ohca.``
``Wow, that's amazing.@k@vS16/00/DS43030181. So even though we're from different countries, you still have a word with the same pronunciation?@k@vS16/00/DS43030182. What does it mean?``
``Ohca means... ``one who links``.``
I remembered the name of my partner.
Ryuun-Ohc.
Ohc means ``link`` in Ryuunish. Its conjugation is ohca.
``One who links, huh...? I like it.``
``That's great! How wonderful that is!@k@vS16/00/DS43030184. Aren't you glad you've got such a lovely name, Ouka-chan?!``
Shino excitedly poked Ouka's squishy cheek.
Ouka.
The one who links Riku and I.
The one who links Shino and I.
And... if possible, I hope she can one day link humans and Ryuun.
I nuzzled my cheek against Ouka's and entrusted my dream to her.
``Ryuun-Ohc.@k@vS16/45/DS43071280. This child... is Ouka.@k@vS16/45/DS43071281. Can you see her?``
``Yes, clearly.@k@vS16/00/DS43050290. ...She looks a lot like you. Her eyes are so clear.``
...It was a few days later.
Once I'd finally regained enough strength to walk, I took Ouka and headed for Onigafuchi.
There was only one reason for that... To show Ryuun-Ohc my daughter in person.
Of course, it was possible to show her through the charged crystal, and I'd already done just that.
But since we'd spent so many years as a peerless pair, I felt she deserved better...
So I asked to bring Ouka into Ryuun-Ohc as well, though I knew it was an unreasonable request.
``I'm sorry for bringing in a foreign body.``
``She is no foreign body, Hanyuu.@k@vS16/00/DS43050292. So long as she has even the slightest of Ryuun blood, she's hardly an impediment on the synchronization's processing.``
``I see.``
``...And besides...``
Ryuun-Ohc hesitated for a moment...
Then informed me of a certain truth.
``This will likely be... the last time.@k@vS16/00/DS43050296. So I am truly glad I could welcome another Ryuun besides you... while I am still functioning normally.``
``It will not be long before I reach my operational limit.@k@vS16/00/DS43050298. I have supported you for many years now, but unfortunately, it would seem this is farewell.``
I was shocked... But there was also a part of me who'd been prepared for this day to come eventually.
The Lynoses across the land were already deteriorating and shutting down one after another.
Which meant that Ryuun-Ohc would be no exception...
``...I'll miss you.@k@vS16/45/DS43071287. In the end, I couldn't do anything to reward you.@k@vS16/45/DS43071288. Instead, you're the one who was always helping and teaching me... Forgive me.``
``That is not true, Hanyuu.@k@vS16/00/DS43050300. ...No, since this is the last time, please allow me to call you this.@k@vS16/00/DS43050301. Yeasomul Jedha.``
That voice. That timbre.
...I'd heard it before.
No way...
Ryuun-Ohc, you're... no, you are...?!
``It is just as you have surmised.@k@vS16/00/DS43050303. I inherited a portion of the personality of the former Yeasomul Jedha... your mother.``
``Thus, it was my role to protect you, admonish you on occasion, and guide you down the right path.``
...Come to think of it, I'd heard about this from the elders.
That there were occasionally Putus who could become an astral body upon death and retain their consciousness and personality.
That while they are fated to vanish eventually, they can temporarily impose their will on the outside world by parasitizing off of other creatures and similar things.
...Which meant that my mother had been dwelling in the pseudo lifeform that was this Lynos for a while after her death?
That part of her personality had been implanted on Ryuun-Ohc, and she'd been watching over me all this time without revealing her true identity, all the while I believed I was all alone?
``N-no way... Then why?``
``I apologize for keeping quiet about my identity.@k@vS16/00/DS43050306. But this was a directive from your mother.``

``In order to keep your consciousness from being solely directed at me...@k@vS16/00/DS43050308. Had you known my true identity, you surely would have relied on me more and made no effort to take awareness of anything else...``
She was right.
Since I'd believed her to have no relation to me, I didn't unilaterally depend on her.

No matter how much I was reasonably admonished and warned, I was able to keep myself from blindly accepting them, able to constantly believe in my will and convictions, and able to keep standing tall on my own two feet.
``...@k@vS16/45/DS43071293. Sorry.@k@vS16/45/DS43071294. No... Please forgive me, Mother.@k@vS16/45/DS43071295. I... I never...``
``It is all right, Yeasomul.@k@vS16/00/DS43050310. You truly have worked so hard up until now.@k@vS16/00/DS43050311. We are proud of you from the bottom of our hearts.``
``...Which is why we want you to keep living on, while treasuring your own happiness.``
I nodded over and over as I felt something hot stream down my cheeks.
...Just then, my sword was suddenly enveloped in light and became a dazzling sphere.
Then it gently floated in front of me before flying off towards the collection of lights shining in the distance, and eventually disappeared into them.
``This sword... has long aided you as an extension of myself...@k@vS16/00/DS43050314. but you likely no longer need it as you are now.@k@vS16/00/DS43050315. Therefore, I have taken it back.``
I was a little sad to feel my sheath growing light.
...But even so, that was a necessary ritual for me to cut ties with the Ryuun and live as a human of this world.
But above all else, it was proof that I'd found a reason for living beyond the way of the sword...
``After I cease operations, part of the Lynos' functions will remain.@k@vS16/00/DS43050317. Should you ever need it, this sword will respond to your summons.``
``...But if possible, I pray that it never comes to that.``
``Mother...``
``Now I have no further regrets.@k@vS16/00/DS43050320. Yeasomul... No, Hanyuu.@k@vS16/00/DS43050321. Please, be happy.``
``! P-please wait, Mother!``
I frantically called out to her when I sensed that voice fading.
Not yet...
There was one thing I needed to tell her... as a symbol of my gratitude.

``The truth is... Riku thought of a name for you.@k@vS16/45/DS43071300_1. Much like he did for me!@k@vS16/45/DS43071301. I apologize for doing this at our final meeting, but would you please hear it?``
``...Of course.``
````Ryuuou``.@k@vS16/45/DS43071303. That is the name of my comrade... and mother.``
``...It's a good name, isn't it?``
``Yes... It's wonderful.@k@vS16/00/DS43050324. Thank you, Hanyuu... I pray for your eternal happiness and health...``
Eventually, we returned to the banks of Onigafuchi.
The sun had already set and it had gotten dark... All that remained were the cries of crickets in the distance.
I held Ouka tightly in my arms and sobbed... Ouka looked up at me with a gentle, comforting expression.
...I wasn't alone.
I'd thought I was all alone up until I met Riku... but I'd always been protected by kindness so vast and gentle.
I was happy.
As well as embarrassed.
By the old me who had struggled all by herself to live as a Ryuun and Jedha, and suffered all on her own accord.
Humans can't live alone. That providence extends to Ryuun as well.
So...
I would pass on these lessons and feelings... to my daughter Ouka.
You're not alone.
There are always people there for you who rejoice and celebrate your existence... People who believe you are irreplaceable and innocently wish for your happiness.
So... don't lose yourself. And don't doubt yourself.
Because there are at least two who are overjoyed that you exist...
Gently combing aside Ouka's bangs, I looked up at the sky on a sudden impulse.
I was happy.
In that moment, happier than anyone else in the entire world.
And those blissful days...
...came to a sudden, unceremonious end.
